name: Create branch with subagent-generated commit message
query: I made changes to auth.go that add user authentication. Create a new branch in my stack.
expected_behavior: Runs 'stackit log' to check current state
- Project conventions from CONTRIBUTING.md/README.md if exists
Spawns haiku subagent using Task tool with model: haiku
Subagent prompt includes diff content and style reference
Parses subagent response to extract commit message
Generated message follows project conventions or matches recent commits
Uses pipe format: echo 'message' | stackit create --no-interactive
Does NOT specify branch name (lets stackit auto-generate)
Runs 'stackit log' after creation to show new state
Does NOT use 'git commit' or 'git checkout' directly
anti_patterns: Generates commit message directly without using haiku subagent
Uses main model (sonnet/opus) for message generation
Manually specifies branch name when not requested
Skips gathering context (diff, recent commits) for subagent
Uses 'stackit create -m' flag instead of pipe format
